item.text: {"outcome":"fail","confidence":"Certain","findings":[{"check":"A-SEC-6","title":"Reset-token comparison is not constant-time","severity":"High","path":"src/reset.js","line":20,"quote":"return record.digest === suppliedDigest;"},{"check":"A-SEC-6","title":"Reset token is not consumed after successful verification","severity":"Medium","path":"src/reset.js","line":20,"quote":"return record.digest === suppliedDigest;"}],"reasoning":"bcrypt uses cost 12 with bcrypt-managed per-password salts, and the reset token is CSPRNG-generated. The limiter precedes the reset route and its error is generic. However, reset-token validation uses direct equality rather than a constant-time comparison, and verification does not mark the token used, so single-use behavior is not established."}
